mission.title: Install and Configure Interlink on Kubernetes
mission.description: InterLink provides an abstraction layer for executing Kubernetes pods on remote resources, enabling the development of provider-specific plugins for the Kubernetes Virtual Kubelet interface. Installing InterLink allows you to leverage its capabilities for managing container execution lifecycles across various environments.

steps.title: Create the InterLink Namespace
steps.description: First, create a namespace for InterLink to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Apply InterLink Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description: Apply the necessary CRDs for InterLink.

steps.title: Deploy InterLink Components
steps.description: Deploy the InterLink components using the provided manifests.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check if the InterLink pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of InterLink will show the pods running in the 'interlink' namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace interlink`. Additionally, you will have configured resource limits and set up TLS for secure communication.

uninstall.title: Remove InterLink Components
uninstall.description: Delete the deployed resources and CRDs for InterLink.

uninstall.title: Delete the InterLink Namespace
uninstall.description: Remove the namespace created for InterLink.

upgrade.title: Upgrade InterLink
upgrade.description: To upgrade InterLink, apply the new version manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pods Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the logs of the pods to identify issues.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that your Kubernetes cluster has enough resources (CPU, memory) to run InterLink.

troubleshooting.title: CRDs Not Found
troubleshooting.description: If CRDs are not found, ensure they are applied correctly and check for any errors during the apply step.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
